evals.prompt:
I've just run match-schema on my nearby brown dwarf astrometry catalog and everything matched cleanly. Here's the mapping:

| Input Column | Description | Units | Type | DB Table | DB Field | Confidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| source_id | Primary source identifier | — | str | Sources | source | High | Name pattern maps to primary identifier |
| ra | Right ascension (J2000) | deg | float64 | Sources | ra_deg | High | Name + units match |
| dec | Declination (J2000) | deg | float64 | Sources | dec_deg | High | Name + units match |
| plx | Parallax | mas | float64 | Parallaxes | parallax_mas | High | Name + units match |
| e_plx | Parallax uncertainty | mas | float64 | Parallaxes | parallax_error | High | Error prefix variant |
| pmRA | Proper motion in RA | mas/yr | float64 | ProperMotions | pm_ra | High | Name + units match |
| e_pmRA | Uncertainty in pmRA | mas/yr | float64 | ProperMotions | pm_ra_error | High | Error prefix |
| pmDE | Proper motion in Dec | mas/yr | float64 | ProperMotions | pm_dec | High | Name + units match |
| e_pmDE | Uncertainty in pmDE | mas/yr | float64 | ProperMotions | pm_dec_error | High | Error prefix |
| ref | Publication reference | — | str | Publications | reference | High | Name + description match |

The validation report came back clean — no nullable violations, no type mismatches. Can you generate the Felis YAML schema for this? Call it 'BrownDwarfAstrometry'.
evals.expected_output: A valid Felis YAML schema file saved to /tmp/BrownDwarfAstrometry-schema.yaml. The schema should: (1) have name: BrownDwarfAstrometry at the top level; (2) include tables for Sources, Parallaxes, ProperMotions, and Publications; (3) use correct Felis syntax including @id references in the format #TableName.columnName; (4) set nullable: false for primary key columns (source, reference); (5) include fits:tunit for columns with units (deg, mas, mas/yr); (6) include foreign key constraints linking source fields back to Sources.source and reference fields back to Publications.reference; (7) also print the full YAML in a code block in the chat.

evals.prompt:
Here's the match-schema output for my radial velocity survey of M dwarfs. Most columns matched, but a few didn't:

| Input Column | Description | Units | Type | DB Table | DB Field | Confidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| designation | 2MASS designation | — | str | Sources | source | High | Primary identifier |
| rv | Heliocentric radial velocity | km/s | float64 | RadialVelocities | rv_kms | High | Name + units match |
| e_rv | RV uncertainty | km/s | float64 | RadialVelocities | rv_error | High | Error prefix |
| sn_ratio | Signal-to-noise ratio of the spectrum | — | float32 | — | — | — | Unmatched: no SN ratio field in schema |
| obs_date | Date of observation (MJD) | d | float64 | — | — | — | Unmatched: observation date not in schema |
| instrument | Instrument used | — | str | — | — | — | Unmatched: no direct instrument field on RadialVelocities |
| ref | Publication reference | — | str | Publications | reference | High | Standard reference field |

For the unmatched columns: add sn_ratio and obs_date as new fields in the RadialVelocities table, and skip the instrument column entirely. Please generate the Felis YAML schema for this — name it 'MDwarfRV'.
evals.expected_output: A valid Felis YAML schema file saved to /tmp/MDwarfRV-schema.yaml. The schema should: (1) include the RadialVelocities table with all matched columns (rv_kms, rv_error, source, reference) PLUS the two new user-requested fields (sn_ratio as float or double, obs_date as double with fits:tunit: d); (2) NOT include the instrument column; (3) include the Sources and Publications tables with appropriate columns; (4) use correct @id syntax throughout; (5) include foreign key constraints for source and reference; (6) summarize which columns were added vs skipped in the chat.

evals.prompt:
I ran both match-schema and validate-schema-mapping on my photometry catalog. Here's what I've got:

**Mapping:**
| Input Column | Description | Units | Type | DB Table | DB Field | Confidence | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| source | Source name | — | str | Sources | source | High | Primary identifier |
| Jmag | J-band magnitude | mag | float32 | Photometry | magnitude | High | Band = 2MASS.J |
| eJmag | J-band uncertainty | mag | float32 | Photometry | magnitude_error | High | Error on Jmag |
| band | Filter identifier | — | str | Photometry | band | High | Maps to PhotometryFilters.band |
| ref | Reference | — | str | Publications | reference | High | Standard reference |

**Validation report:**
## Schema Mapping Validation Report

### Nullable Violations (1 issue)
| Data Column | Maps To | Null Count | Total Rows | % Null |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| eJmag | Photometry.magnitude_error | 23 | 500 | 4.6% |

### Type Mismatches (0 issues)

### Summary
- 1 nullable violation: eJmag has nulls but magnitude_error is non-nullable in the template schema
- 0 type mismatches
- 4 columns passed cleanly

For the nullable violation: please mark magnitude_error as nullable in the new schema (some objects don't have error estimates). Generate the Felis YAML — name it 'Photometry2MASSJ'.
evals.expected_output: A valid Felis YAML schema file saved to /tmp/Photometry2MASSJ-schema.yaml. The schema should: (1) include Sources, Photometry, PhotometryFilters, and Publications tables; (2) critically, the magnitude_error column in Photometry must have nullable: true (or omit nullable entirely) — NOT nullable: false — because the user chose to allow nulls for the eJmag column; (3) use float or double for magnitude and magnitude_error columns (based on float32 input type); (4) include foreign key constraints; (5) note in the chat that magnitude_error was made nullable per user request due to 4.6% null rate.
